# familio_marriage (Resource)

A marriage between two persons in a familio.org family tree, modelled as the wedding event that links the partners. Changing the partners or the marriage date forces replacement (event editing is not yet supported).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A marriage between two existing persons (a "wedding" event under the hood).
resource "familio_marriage" "marriage" {
  partners = [familio_person.ivan.uuid, familio_person.maria.uuid]

  marriage_date = {
    year  = 1875
    month = 5
    day   = 12
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `partners` (Set of String) UUIDs of the two partner persons. Both must already exist.

### Optional

- `marriage_date` (Attributes) Marriage date. Changing it forces a new resource (event editing is not yet supported). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--marriage_date))

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `updated_at` (String) Last update timestamp.
- `uuid` (String) The underlying wedding-event UUID.

<a id="nestedatt--marriage_date"></a>
### Nested Schema for `marriage_date`

Required:

- `year` (Number) Year (e.g. 1900).

Optional:

- `day` (Number) Day of month, 1-31.
- `month` (Number) Month, 1-12.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# familio_marriage is imported by "<partner_person_uuid>:<wedding_event_uuid>"
# (a partner anchors the lookup; the full partner set is read back from the event).
terraform import familio_marriage.marriage 121681c3-9087-4486-b7e3-0774e346d229:ed0a313b-28ac-4e0a-bb3e-edad4430549a
```
